method_atom: a statistically optimal estimator that pools samples drawn from several distinct probability distributions related by known reweighting factors and solves a coupled set of self-consistent estimating equations to recover the ratios of their normalizing constants and weighted expectation values. It operates directly on continuous samples rather than binned histograms, thereby avoiding discretization bias, and in the large-sample limit is asymptotically unbiased with the minimum variance of any such reweighting estimator while supplying uncertainty estimates for every quantity. It generalizes a two-distribution acceptance-ratio estimator to the many-distribution case.
object_atom: free energy differences and ensemble-averaged observables of physical systems sampled at multiple equilibrium (Boltzmann-weighted) states, illustrated by the potential of mean force of a DNA hairpin reconstructed from optical-tweezer pulling measurements under constant force bias.
brief_reason: The multi-distribution variance-minimal reweighting/normalizing-constant-ratio estimator is the transferable machinery, while the equilibrium thermodynamic states and the DNA-hairpin free-energy application are the domain-specific object.
